title: thevetia ahouai
intro: Thevetia ahouai, also known as the yellow oleander, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Thevetia ahouai has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of cardiac glycosides and other compounds. The health benefits of Thevetia ahouai include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and a purgative. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and it is also used as a tincture or in the form of a powder for various herbal preparations, although it should be used with caution due to its potential toxicity.
